questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some mangoes are nuts. Some nuts are fruits. Conclusions: I. Some mangoes are fruits. II. Some fruits are not mangoes.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some kites are horses. All horses are dogs. Conclusions: I. All dogs are horses. II. Some dogs are not horses.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All beds are sheets. Some towels are sheets. Conclusions: I. Some towels are beds. II. Some sheets are not towels.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: No boy is a thief. Some men are thieves. Conclusions: I. Some men are not boys. II. Some thieves are not boys.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All dresses are shoes. No shoe is brown. Conclusions: I. No dresses are brown. II. Some shoes are dresses.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some hens are cows. All cows are horses. Conclusions: I. Some horses are hens. II. Some hens are horses.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some chairs are stools. Table is a chair. Conclusions: I. Some stools are chairs. II. Table is not a stool.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some dogs bark. All dogs bite. Conclusions: I. Even those dogs who do not bark, bite. II. Those dogs who do not bark, do not necessarily bite.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All poets are readers. No reader is wise. Conclusions: I. No poet is wise. II. All readers are poets.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some trees are houses. Biscuit is a tree. Conclusions: I. Biscuit is not a tree. II. Some houses are trees.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All cows are Rambha. Some buffaloes are Rambha. Conclusions: I. All Rambhas are not cows. II. Some Rambhas are not buffaloes.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some rooms are stones. All stones are radios. Conclusions: I. Some rooms are radios. II. Some stones are rooms.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some gowns are shirts. Some shirts are trousers. Conclusions: I. Some gowns are trousers. II. Some trousers are shirts.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some books are cars. Some cars are boxes. Conclusions: I. Some books are boxes. II. Some boxes are books.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some dogs are horses. No horse is black. Conclusions: I. Some dogs are black. II. Some horses are dogs.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All trucks fly. Some scooters fly. Conclusions: I. All trucks are scooters. II. Some scooters do not fly.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All businessmen are hardworking. No hardworking man is superstitious. Conclusions: I. No businessman is superstitious. II. All superstitions are not businessmen.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All educated people read newspapers. Rahul does not read newspaper. Conclusions: I. Rahul is not educated. II. Reading newspaper is not essential to be educated.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All poets are authors. All singers are authors. Conclusions: I. All singers are poets. II. Some authors are not singers.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Most crops are machines. Some machines are fools. Conclusions: I. Some fools are machines. II. Some crops are fools.
